questions.q: When was the Olympic Movement founded?
questions.explanation: The Olympic Movement was founded in 1892 by Pierre de Coubertin.

questions.q: Who is the founder of the Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Pierre de Coubertin is widely regarded as the founder of the modern Olympic Games.

questions.q: What is the motto of the Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The motto of the Olympic Games is 'Citius Altius Fortius', which is Latin for 'Faster Higher Stronger'.

questions.q: What is the International Olympic Committee (IOC)?
opts: (A) The governing body of the Olympic Games
(B) The organization responsible for the Paralympic Games
(C) The federation of international sports organizations
questions.explanation: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is the governing body of the Olympic Games.

questions.q: Which of the following is a fundamental principle of the Olympic Charter?
questions.explanation: Universality of participation is one of the fundamental principles of the Olympic Charter.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic torch relay?
questions.explanation: The Olympic torch relay is officially known as the Olympic Flame Relay.

questions.q: Which of the following is a Olympic value?
questions.explanation: Respect, Excellence, and Friendship are the three Olympic values.

questions.q: Who lit the Olympic flame at the 1936 Berlin Olympics?
questions.explanation: Fritz Schilgen lit the Olympic flame at the 1936 Berlin Olympics.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic oath?
questions.explanation: The Olympic oath is officially known as the Olympic Oath.

questions.q: Which of the following is a role of the International Olympic Committee (IOC)?
questions.explanation: The IOC has multiple roles, including governing, promoting, and organizing the Olympic Games.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic Village?
questions.explanation: The Olympic Village is officially known as the Olympic Village.

questions.q: Who is the current President of the International Olympic Committee (IOC)?
questions.explanation: Thomas Bach is the current President of the IOC.

questions.q: Which of the following is a key aspect of the Olympic Movement?
questions.explanation: Competition and sportsmanship are core values of the Olympic Movement.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic flag?
questions.explanation: The Olympic flag is officially known as the Olympic Flag.

questions.q: Who is the founder of the modern Olympic Games in the United States?
questions.explanation: James E. Sullivan is often credited with helping to establish the modern Olympic Games in the United States.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic Charter's governing body?
questions.explanation: The IOC is the governing body of the Olympic Charter.

questions.q: Which of the following is a key principle of the Olympic Movement?
questions.explanation: Universality of participation, excellence, and respect are key principles of the Olympic Movement.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic flame's creator?
questions.explanation: Theodoros Vryzakis created the Olympic flame.

questions.q: Which of the following is a role of the National Olympic Committee?
opts: (C) To select athletes for the Olympic Games
questions.explanation: The National Olympic Committee has multiple roles, including governing, promoting, and selecting athletes for the Olympic Games.

questions.q: What is the name of the Olympic Games' governing body in the United States?
opts: (B) Olympic Council of the United States
(D) Olympic Board of the United States
questions.explanation: The United States Olympic Committee is the governing body of the Olympic Games in the United States.
